title: palaquium gutta var. oblongifolium
intro: Palaquium gutta var. oblongifolium, also known as the Malabar gutta-percha tree, is a plant species belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to help alleviate symptoms of various health issues. The health benefits of Palaquium gutta var. oblongifolium include its potential to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat infections, making it a valuable resource in herbal medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of extracts, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
